commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(horizon-2): XDP_REDIRECT prototype + AF_XDP bridge + hardware spec

Stream 2 — Performance Campaign (post-GA):
- src/network/ebpf/experimental/xdp_redirect_prototype.c: BPF program
  using BPF_MAP_TYPE_DEVMAP + bpf_redirect_map to bypass kernel stack
  for mesh transit traffic on MESH_PORT 26969; per-CPU perf_stats counters
- src/network/ebpf/experimental/afxdp_bridge.go: Go bridge prototype
  linking to pinned XSKMAP; UMEM allocation pending i40e/ixgbe hardware
- docs/research/HORIZON2_HARDWARE_SPEC.md: dual-NIC testbed spec
  (Intel X710-DA2 / X520-DA2 / mlx5), DAC cable topology, pktgen setup
- docs/research/RFC_AF_XDP_INTEGRATION.md: XDP_REDIRECT + AF_XDP RFC,
  XSKMAP design, zero-copy vs copy-mode, Go integration, honest gate:
  1M+ PPS requires Native XDP on i40e/ixgbe, not achievable on virtio

fix(ci): use GITHUB_WORKFLOW_REF for keyless verify-blob identity

The hardcoded --certificate-identity referenced the wrong org
(x0tta6bl4 vs x0tta6bl4-ai) and wrong workflow file (ci.yml vs
ebpf-release-signing.yml). Use GITHUB_WORKFLOW_REF which GitHub
Actions sets automatically to the exact subject embedded in the
keyless OIDC certificate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire briefing generator through full SDK session pipeline (#86)

* feat: wire briefing generator through full SDK session pipeline

Replace the single-turn "summarize this context string" pattern with
the same prepareSdkSession + runSdkSession infrastructure that meetings
and commissions use. The Guild Master worker now gets its identity,
memory, and read-only tools (Read, Glob, Grep) to explore .lore/ for
plans, specs, issues, and notes before producing a briefing.

Key changes:
- Add "briefing" context type across SDK runner, toolbox resolver, and
  base toolbox type unions
- Guard toolbox resolver context factory lookup so missing entries
  (like "briefing") don't crash
- Strip manager system toolbox for briefings (read-only, no coordination tools)
- Replace TTL-only cache with HEAD-commit + TTL: cache is valid when the
  integration worktree HEAD hasn't moved AND the entry is under 1 hour old
- Force includePartialMessages in runSdkSession (the event translator
  requires stream_event messages for text extraction) and remove the
  field from SessionPrepSpec/SdkQueryOptions so callers can't misconfigure it
- Add collectRunnerText() helper for SdkRunnerEvent streams
- Prompt now directs exploration of .lore/ subdirectories and scales
  response length to project activity level
- Backwards-compatible: queryFn without prepDeps falls back to single-turn

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: preserve newlines in briefing text display

Add white-space: pre-line to .briefingText so \n characters
from the SDK-generated briefing render as line breaks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: credit winnings atomically instead of chain sync overwrite

syncBalanceFromChain after bet resolution was overwriting lockFunds
deductions for concurrent in-flight bets, causing balance loss.

Now: indexer calls creditWinnings(winner, payout) atomically after
forfeitLocked. No chain→DB sync during resolution — DB is source of truth.

commit.message:
fix(ci): remove explicit SIGSTORE_ID_TOKEN check in keyless signing

cosign uses GitHub Actions ambient OIDC credentials automatically when
the workflow has id-token: write — passing --identity-token manually
is redundant and caused the workflow to fail because the env var was
never explicitly exported.

fix: vocab translations, home navigation after lesson

- lessonStore: add vocabTranslations map + setVocabTranslation action
- advanceFromGrammar: fetch PT-BR translation alongside image for each
  vocabulary word so the card shows the real translation, not the word
  itself
- vocab rendering: use stored translation (falls back to word if fetch
  failed)

  as its own placeholder
- finishLesson: replace store.setPhase('complete') with store.reset() +

fix: Batch 32 — Excel formula parity (insolvencia-sk, konsolidace-povinnost, konto-pracovni-doby)

- insolvencia-sk: replace frozen r.celkovoSplatene.toFixed(2) with calcRow cell ref B13
- konsolidace-povinnost: fix invalid '500M'/'1B' literals → 500000000/1000000000; fix B10→B11 cell ref

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite stock-details API with robust fallback strategy

Complete rewrite to fix VOO/QQQ failures:

NEW STRATEGY:
=============
1. Primary: Use chart endpoint (v8/chart) - MOST RELIABLE
   - Always works for price data
   - Returns price, name, volume, currency
   - Used in /api/stocks successfully

2. Enrichment: Try quote endpoint (v7/quote) - BEST EFFORT
   - Non-blocking, won't fail the request
   - Adds marketCap, P/E, EPS, dividend, beta, 52w high/low
   - If fails, we still have basic data from step 1

3. Graceful degradation:
   - Always returns at least price data
   - Never fails completely
   - Metrics show 0 if enrichment fails (vs crashing)

IMPROVEMENTS:
=============
- Better headers (Referer, Origin from yahoo.com)
- Longer timeout for chart (15s)
- Shorter timeout for enrichment (8s, non-critical)
- Extract latest price from closes array if meta missing
- Extract latest volume from volumes array if meta missing
- Better null handling throughout

DEBUG API:
==========
- Added /api/test-yahoo endpoint
- Tests all 3 Yahoo Finance endpoints
- Returns full response for debugging
- Usage: /api/test-yahoo?symbol=VOO

This ensures VOO, QQQ, and all other symbols will at least
show price data even if full metrics are unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: make homepage intro prose CMS-driven (#31)

Seeds a 'home' slug in site_content_pages containing the landing page
intro text. LandingPage fetches it via usePublicContentPage and renders
it with MarkdownRenderer, replacing the previously hardcoded paragraphs.

If the content request fails or is still loading, the prose section is
simply omitted — the events list and the rest of the page are unaffected.
The text is editable by admins via the existing /admin/content editor.

commit.message:
Fix readTime in all articles: 215 wpm formula (was manually guessed)

Correct calculation: Math.ceil(wordCount / 215) + ' min read'
- blackout-survival-guide: 557 words → 3 min read (was: 11 min read)
- bug-out-bag-checklist:   591 words → 3 min read (was: 14 min read)
- emergency-water-filters: 594 words → 3 min read (was:  9 min read)
- wildfire-evacuation-plan: 526 words → 3 min read (was:  7 min read)

Formula saved in CLAUDE.md for all future articles.

Add Module 7: Before You React — using AI to analyze heated exchanges

New module teaches using AI as a pause button before reacting to charged
conversations. Draws on layered critical analysis (fallacy detection,

and adds _site/ to .gitignore.

commit.message:
Fix hero bento grid visibility — remove opacity bottleneck

The decoIn animation was capping block opacity at 0.7 via var(--o),
and the container had opacity 0.85, making the translucent blocks
nearly invisible against the light hero background. Set both to 1
so the rgba fill colors control the actual visual opacity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove IPConfiguration from network interface — conflicts with ip= kernel arg

The Firecracker SDK rejects configs where both IPConfiguration and the
"ip=" kernel boot arg are set. Since we use "ip=" for simplicity, drop
IPConfiguration from StaticNetworkConfiguration entirely.

commit.message:
fix: Tidal pagination using count API instead of page size heuristic

Tidal API may return fewer items than `limit` without being at the end,
causing premature pagination termination (97 tracks instead of thousands).
Now uses get_tracks_count/get_albums_count/get_artists_count to determine
real totals before paginating.

commit.message:
Improve UX: auto-render, notification dot, guided tour, processing overlay

1. Auto-Render: Pyodide runner now scans all globals for any plotly
   Figure instance — not just _fig — so charts appear automatically
   regardless of what the user names their variable.

2. Visual Cue: Visualizer tab shows a pulsing green dot (animate-ping)
   whenever a chart is produced while the user is on the Editor tab.
   Dot clears when they switch to the Visualizer.

3. One-Click Help: Empty visualizer panel shows a "How do I see my
   chart?" button that launches a 5-step guided tour (GuidedTour.tsx).
   Each step auto-advances every 1 second with a progress bar; steps
   can be skipped or the tour dismissed at any time.

4. Processing Overlay: A floating pill (ProcessingOverlay.tsx) appears
   at the bottom of the screen whenever the WASM engine is running.
   Shows row count for datasets >1 000 rows and an elapsed-seconds
   counter so users know the app is working, not frozen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Windows SEA build: strip \r from node path

The `where node` command on Windows outputs paths with \r\n line
endings. split('\n')[0] left trailing \r in the path, causing
ENOENT on copyFile.
